label: Rerank Chunks
description: Re-order a list of retrieved chunks by relevance to a query using a deterministic lexical relevance scorer (BM25-style term-overlap with IDF weighting + phrase-match boost). No model round-trip on the default path, so it is deterministic and harness-friendly. Optionally an LLM cross-encoder mode can be enabled, but the deterministic scorer is the default and is what RAG flows use to tighten the candidate set before synthesis. Wire it AFTER knowledge_search / rag_query and BEFORE llm_completion so the model sees the most relevant chunks first (and only the top-N).

settings.name: query
settings.label: Query
settings.description: The user question / search query the chunks should be ranked against. Term-overlap with this query drives the relevance score.

settings.name: chunksPath
settings.label: Chunks path on input
settings.description: Dot-path into the incoming edge's input object where the chunk array lives (e.g. `results` for knowledge_search, `result.results` for the nested envelope). The executor also auto-detects common shapes (`input.results`, `input.result.results`, `input.chunks`, or `input` itself when it is already an array). Each chunk should carry a `content`/`text` field (and optionally an existing `score`).

settings.name: topN
settings.label: Top N
settings.description: Keep only the top-N chunks after reranking. Trims a wide candidate set down to what the synthesis step actually needs.

settings.name: contentField
settings.label: Content field
settings.description: Which field on each chunk holds the text to score. Falls back to `text`, then `body`, then the chunk stringified.

ai.shortDescription: Re-rank retrieved chunks by lexical relevance to the query (deterministic, no model call by default), keep top-N.
ai.whenToUse: In a RAG flow, between the retriever (knowledge_search / rag_query) and the synthesizer (llm_completion). Cross-encoder rerank tightens recall@k into precision@k so the model isn't drowned in marginally-relevant chunks.
ai.examplePrompt: rerank the retrieved chunks against the user's question and keep the top 3
ai.promptHints: Set `query` to the user's question (templated) and leave chunksPath default to read knowledge_search's `results`. Reference the reordered set via {{steps.<id>.result.chunks}} downstream.

outputAssertions.name: rerank_result_shape
outputAssertions.expression: result && typeof result.query === 'string' && Array.isArray(result.chunks) && typeof result.outputCount === 'number'
outputAssertions.errorMessage: rerank result must be { query, chunks[], inputCount, outputCount, reordered, method }.
